sandboxFiles.name: Copy of A New Drive Agenda
sandboxFiles.content:
6/12
* Why are we doing this?
   * We believe the power of AI & Agents will transform the way people work. 
   * Most users are not making the most of AI & agents in Drive today
      * 0.7% AI attach rate for AI Mode / Projects
      * < 0.1% AI attach rate for Help Me Organize
      * < 1% (engaged) AI attach rate for AIO 
   * We want to help as many people as possible unlock the power of AI & Agents to 10x their productivity at work
   * Our initial hypothesis is that by folding AI / Agents in a simple, intuitive way into a familiar construct with broad reach (folders), we will be able to reach more users and deepen the impact
* Our scope: how do we make folders amazing?
   * Should aim to build for the future (thinking about how AI / agents show up) but high value non-AI improvements are fair game too
   * Questions to think about
      * How should we tackle non-location-based groupings (Projects vs. folders)?
      * How do folders containing a web app show up in Drive?
      * How should team members collaborate with agents in a folder? 
         * Context & memory
         * Collaboration model
         * Proactivity 
      * How can users bring in context from outside a folder? 
      * How should creation from a folder work?
      * How should folder organization work? 
      * How should navigation across the file hierarchy work?
      * How can a user see what humans / agents have done in a folder and undo changes if needed?
* Principles: Principles for a new Drive
* In-person summit (late June)  
* Demos
   * Projects doc list
   * Column view
   * Web apps
* Next steps
   * UXR: Share relevant UXR to help inspire (folders / AI / agents)
   * Eng: Catalogue product baggage (why is it hard to build new things)
   * All: Share external / internal products that could serve as inspiration in group chat 
   * All: Take a hypothesis / question you’re passionate about and build a prototype → we’ll structure this meeting around prototypes and discussion
* Appendix
   * Why are we proposing a new Drive?
      * Hypotheses
         * Velocity: we’ll be able to move quicker
         * Boldness: we’ll be able to be more creative if we have a blank canvas
         * Simplify: we won’t have to build for all users from day 1




6/11




mhop@ Thoughts on Goal
* Drive is investing in big rocks right now
   * Projects & AIM
   * Search
   * Agent Friendly Drive
   * Drive for Desktop
   * Gemini in Drive
* Rather than tackling building a new version of Drive, we should probably choose a more concrete problem to tackle / get buy in before thinking about expanding
* Our scope: how do we make folders amazing?
   * Should aim to build for the future (thinking about how AI / agents show up) but high value non-AI improvements are fair game too
   * Questions to think about
      * How should we tackle non-location-based groupings (Projects vs. folders)?
      * How do folders containing a web app show up in Drive?
      * How should team members collaborate with agents in a folder? 
         * Context & memory
         * Collaboration model
         * Proactivity 
      * How can users bring in context from outside a folder? 
      * How should creation from a folder work?
      * How should folder organization work? 
      * How should navigation across the file hierarchy work?
      * How can a user see what humans / agents have done in a folder and undo changes if needed? 
* How we start: Building a prototype (ideally that allows a user to connect with their actual Drive data)
   * We could make our team check-ins informal and either have someone present a prototype for an idea / question and then have a discussion
* Goal: get buy-in from leadership to make investment. At that point in time we decide if we build in existing Drive or start something new




Original agenda
* What is the goal of our workstream?
   * Launch a beta version of Drive that gets 100K+ opt ins
   * Either replace Drive long-term or incorporate learnings back into Drive
* Who are we building for
   * MVP: AI-savvy SMBs
   * Long-term: all of Drive users
* Platform
   * Web?
   * Mobile?
* What is our scope
   * Reimagine folders with AI / agents?
   * Reimagine Drive with AI / agents?
* Principles 
   * Build incrementally: build one concept at a time and add on
   * Keep it simple: build the 20% of features that provide 80% of the value to limit complexity
   * Focus: focus on the parts of the product where we can have the most impact first
* How do we want to approach ideation? 
   * User-journey-based 
      * Store & organize
      * Find & view
      * Create & edit
      * Collaborate
      * Consume
   * Brainstorm proces
      * [T - 2] UXR brief on Drive learnings to date
         * Drive CUJ Benchmarking Round 4 (2022) | go/drive-cujs-2022        
      * [T - 1] Team drops inspirational screenshots of competitor products 
      * [T] Team walks through any prototypes already created
      * [T] Identify what problems we want to solve, or identify open questions 
         * FigJam - I like, I wish, I wonder
         * Lightweight brainstorming in figma & voting / discussion
      * UX puts together designs 
      * Team prototypes ideas (either in AI Studio or an eng built prototype)
      * Share prototype with leads for feedback
      * Catalog the baggage – why (from Eng, Product, or UX) is it hard/slow to make improvements/features in the existing product?

sandboxFiles.name: Copy of Copy of Copy of Copy of Copy of Copy of Game Plan for A New Drive
sandboxFiles.content:
Tab 1
A New Drive


Goal: What is the best way to bring AI to Folders


Team Structure
* UX: Daniel, Emily, Matt
* PM: mhop
* Eng: John + Marcus


Concepts to Explore
* Journeys
   * Find & View
      * Folder navigation
      * File preview
      * Pinned folders
   * Create & Edit
      * Context
      * Interaction models
      * Artifact formats (including web apps)
   * Store
      * Upload artifacts from 3p
      * Organize
   * Collaborate
      * Comments 
      * Liveness (cursors, profile image)
   * Consume 
      * Side panel
      * Search 
* Primitives 
   * Agents
      * Context & memory
      * Collaboration model
      * Proactivity 
   * Folder history
      * Activity history
      * Reverting changes
   * Web app


Process
* Choose concept to focus on for week
* Brainstorm  Jun 11, 2026 ideas as a cross-functional group (UX to lead) we should narrow focus to folder / file nav and do scoped agents the following day
   * Pre-read existing research 
   * Emily to walk through existing work for extra
   * Identify what problems we want to solve, or identify open questions 
      * FigJam - I like, I wish, I wonder
   * Lightweight brainstorming in figma
* Identify key questions & discuss as group
* UX puts together Figma / AI studio prototype
* IC SWEs build concept
* Share updates from the previous week with Drive leadership team




Define principles 


Resources
Sojo Agents PRD
Add existing designs explorations 
* Column view
   * Figma 
   * Finding & organizing sprint Deck
* Projector - Version History
* Editor Comments 
* Proactivity
   * Super Folder - Figma & Deck
* Live Cursor
Average length of file & folder name
